page: Microsoft Azure E64_v5 is a memory-optimized server from the Ev5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs.

In benchmark testing, the E64_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by its high scores in multi-threaded stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory performance is robust, with strong uncached large-block read and write bandwidths of 286.33 GB/sec and 130.34 GB/sec respectively. Single-core performance remains in the average tier. The server excels in database operations, static web serving throughput at 53.72 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations, which reached 60.83 million operations per second.

While the server exhibits strong capabilities in memory-intensive tasks, software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression, it shows limitations in large-scale AI workloads. LLM inference for text generation using a large 70B model is weak, yielding only 2.74 tokens per second. Given the high memory density and lack of local storage, this instance is highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that utilize remote storage, such as large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and enterprise-grade web serving.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Ev5 family, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, but includes no local storage or GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, particularly in large-block uncached operations. Benchmark results highlight its strength in database operations, Redis SET operations (60.83 million ops/sec), and static web serving (53.72 GB/sec), though single-core execution and large LLM inference speeds are average to weak. This profile makes the E64_v5 highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, and enterprise web serving workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64_v5 is an Ev5 family memory-optimized server featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM. It delivers strong multi-core and database performance under Hyper-V virtualization.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64_v5 is a memory-optimized server with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized configuration featuring sixty-four virtual cores and half a terabyte of RAM for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
512.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU resources included in the base configuration
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise web serving
